subject: Sociology
title: Mass Incarceration
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Mass Incarceration.

hook.text: The US has 5% of the world's population and 25% of its prisoners. That ratio demands an explanation.

buildup.text: Mass incarceration exploded after the 1970s, driven by the war on drugs and mandatory minimum sentences.

discovery.text: Black Americans are incarcerated at five times the rate of white Americans for comparable offenses.

twist.text: Michelle Alexander calls it the new Jim Crow — racial control through criminal justice rather than formal law.

climax.text: Mass incarceration doesn't just punish crime. It creates a permanent underclass stripped of rights and chances.

punchline.text: Prison became America's answer to inequality.

quiz.question: What drove the rise of mass incarceration in the US?
options: The war on drugs and mandatory minimum sentences
